title: T4: fabric-hint stderr 人-first banner reformat
description: Replace the existing block-reason text in fabric-hint.cjs stderr with banner-style human-readable message designed for the AI client UI (not Agent jussive language). Q-13 resolved: the first reader is the human user, Agent reads incidentally. Q-6 resolved: drop the fabricated '3 candidates detected' framing — the hook has zero content awareness. Include real activity overview from `.fabric/.cache/edit-counter` sidecar: top 2-3 most-edited directories since last archive. Format example (Signal A): '📋 Fabric: 距上次归档已过 24h / 累计 22 次编辑。\n   最近活动集中在: packages/server/services/ (12 edits), packages/cli/ (8 edits).\n   是否调 /fabric-archive 检查值得归档的决策/踩坑/复用?'

files.change: replace `formatBannerForSignal()` implementations: Signal A archive banner uses 人-first format with edit-counter top-N aggregation; remove any 'N candidates detected' wording; reads new threshold names from T7 config
expose `getTopEditedDirectories(n)` helper that returns top-n by edit count since last knowledge_proposed event
update snapshot tests: banner text matches new 人-first format; no occurrence of 'candidates detected' wording; top-directory aggregation appears when edit-counter has data

implementation: Read T7 config-loader helper to fetch `archive_hint_hours` and edit-count threshold
Implement `getTopEditedDirectories(n)`: parse `.fabric/.cache/edit-counter` (existing sidecar from rc.6), group edits by 2-level directory prefix, sort by count desc, return top n
Refactor `formatBannerForSignal('archive', ctx)` to produce the new banner (use template literal; include emoji prefix; line 1 = elapsed + edit count; line 2 = top-N activity; line 3 = action prompt)
Remove any code path referencing 'candidates detected' or similar content-aware framing
Update Signal B (review) and Signal C (import) banners similarly to 人-first style for consistency
Update jest/vitest snapshots; verify no Agent-jussive wording remains

reference.pattern: rc.6 edit-counter sidecar at `.fabric/.cache/edit-counter` (line-delimited JSON of {ts, file_path})
reference.examples: Banner example provided in scope Section 2 (B1)

criteria: GIVEN edit-counter has 22 entries (12 in packages/server/services/, 8 in packages/cli/) AND ≥24h since last knowledge_proposed WHEN fabric-hint.cjs runs THEN stderr contains the 3-line banner with '22 次编辑' AND 'packages/server/services/ (12 edits)' AND 'packages/cli/ (8 edits)'
stderr text contains NO occurrence of the string 'candidates detected' (case insensitive) or any other content-aware framing
Banner uses 人-first language (问句 '是否调 /fabric-archive') NOT Agent-jussive ('Invoke /fabric-archive')
Snapshot tests pass for Signal A, B, C banner formats

rationale.chosen_approach: Activity-only signal (no content-awareness claim) + 人-first banner — honest about what the hook knows, polite-question framing matches first-reader (human)
alternatives_considered: Drop edit-counter aggregation, just show time elapsed
decision_factors: Q-6 honesty (hook has no content awareness)
Activity overview gives user enough signal to decide whether to invoke archive Skill
rationale.tradeoffs: Banner is longer (3 lines vs 1) — accepted, line breaks aid scanning

manual_checks: Trigger Stop hook in real session after 24h+ → visually confirm banner appears in Claude Code/Cursor/Codex stderr area
Verify edit-counter aggregation matches actual file edits
success_metrics: Banner rendering ≤3 lines + emoji prefix on all 3 clients
No 'candidates detected' string in any test fixture or production stderr output
unit: getTopEditedDirectories returns correct top-n for known counter input
formatBannerForSignal('archive', ctx) snapshot matches expected template
integration: End-to-end: simulated Stop hook with mocked edit-counter and time → assert stderr text shape
